title: Monetary Policy Report—July 2025
summary: The Bank of Canada maintained its overnight rate target at 2.75% (Bank Rate at 3%, deposit rate at 2.70%) in July 2025, as US tariffs remain significantly elevated and trade policy uncertainty continues to cloud Canada's economic outlook. Canadian economic activity has slowed considerably due to the trade conflict but is showing signs of resilience, while underlying inflation has risen to approximately 2.5%.
key_points: Bank of Canada held target overnight rate at 2.75% with Bank Rate at 3% and deposit rate at 2.70%
US tariffs are significantly higher than at start of 2025; US trade policy remains unpredictable
Canadian economic activity has slowed considerably due to trade conflict but is showing signs of resilience
Headline inflation near 2% target, but underlying inflation has risen to about 2.5%
Global growth has slowed since early 2025 amid ongoing shifts in US trade policy and resulting uncertainty
Considerable uncertainty surrounds inflation outlook; trade conflict escalation or de-escalation would alter economic outcomes
why_it_matters: This report provides the Bank of Canada's comprehensive assessment of the Canadian economic outlook during an active US-Canada trade conflict. The decision to hold rates steady reflects a cautious approach—recognizing economic slowdown from tariffs while monitoring elevated underlying inflation. Market participants track these reports for signals on the future rate path and to understand how trade policy is affecting the Canadian economy.
macro_context: The trade conflict with the US is the dominant macro theme. US tariffs have risen to levels not seen in decades, weighing on Canadian exports and overall economic activity. The Bank notes that the range of trade policy outcomes has narrowed with some new tariff agreements in place, but the situation remains fluid. Underlying inflation at 2.5% gives the Bank room to hold rates steady while growth slows.

issues_found: Source body contains only the overview/introductory section of the Monetary Policy Report; the full PDF report with detailed projections and analysis is not included in the source text. Key figures are accurately extracted from the overview, but more detailed policy deliberations are not available for verification.

Source published July 30, 2025—approximately 8 months before TODAY (2026-03-31). While monetary policy reports have clear archival value as historical policy documents, this age reduces confidence slightly.

human_review.notes: Record is accurate and well-constructed against available source text. Minor uncertainty arises from (1) source body being an overview rather than full report, and (2) the 8-month age of the document. Neither issue requires human intervention—the record is correctly formatted and factual claims are supported by the source.

notes: Source is a Bank of Canada Monetary Policy Report for July 2025—historical document as of TODAY (2026-03-31). The source body contains the overview section of the full report; the complete report with detailed projections and analysis is available in PDF format at the source URL. Governing Council members cited: Tiff Macklem, Carolyn Rogers, Toni Gravelle, Sharon Kozicki, Nicolas Vincent, Rhys Mendes, and Michelle Alexopoulos.
